title: dryopteris carthusiana subsp. dilatata
intro: Dryopteris carthusiana subsp. dilatata, also known as the dilated wood fern, is a subspecies of fern belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae, characterized by its distinctive fronds and woodland habitats. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its chemical composition, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Dryopteris carthusiana subsp. dilatata have been associated with various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or tincture, and its extracts have been incorporated into topical creams and ointments to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
